title: Lastreopsis nephrodioides

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastreopsis nephrodioides include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. taxonomy: Lastreopsis nephrodioides is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Lastreopsis, which is part of the Thelypteridaceae family within the Thelypteridales order. This species is native to Australia and other parts of the world, where it grows in subtropical and tropical regions. The classification of Lastreopsis nephrodioides places it within the kingdom Plantae, specifically the division Pteridophyta and the phylum Pteridophyta.
